text:
To make 1 liter of juice, Sam needs 5 kilograms of oranges. Therefore, for 4 liters of juice, Sam would need:

\[ 5 \text{ kg/liter} \times 4 \text{ liters} = 20 \text{ kg} \]

Each kilogram of oranges costs $3. To find out the total cost for 20 kilograms of oranges, we calculate:

\[ 20 \text{ kg} \times 3 \text{ dollars/kg} = 60 \text{ dollars} \]

Thus, Sam would have to spend $60 to make 4 liters of juice.
